question.text: An InvIT has assets valued at Rs. 10,000 crore. If it wants to borrow Rs. 5,500 crore (55% of asset value), which of the following conditions must be satisfied?

answer.text: Credit rating and unit holder approval only
AAA credit rating, unit holder approval, funds used only for acquisition/development of infrastructure projects, and track record of 6 distributions
Such borrowing is not permitted under InvIT Regulations
AAA credit rating and unit holder approval only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Rs. 5,500 crore is 55% of Rs. 10,000 crore, which falls in the 49-70% range. Under InvIT Regulations, borrowings between 49% and 70% require: (1) AAA credit rating, (2) funds utilized only for acquisition or development of infrastructure projects, (3) approval of unit holders, and (4) track record of 6 distributions. **The Trap:** Credit rating and unit holder approval alone are sufficient only for the 25-49% range. The maximum borrowing limit is 70%, so 55% is permitted with additional conditions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Calculate the percentage first (5,500/10,000 = 55%), identify the tier (49-70%), then apply all four conditions for that tier.
